expressions.english: to wait for a long time
expressions.notes: 오래 = for a long time, a long time

expressions.english: to wait inside
to eagerly look forward to something; to count the days waiting for something
expressions.notes: 손꼽다 = to count something on one's fingers

expressions.english: to wait for a long while
to wait for one's turn
to wait for one's friend
to wait it out; to wait until the end
Have you been waiting for long?
Wait for a moment, please.
expressions.notes:
잠깐 = short time; for a moment
-만 = only; just
-이/어/여 주세요 = (polite) Please do + something + for me

expressions.english: I am waiting for my friend.
I can't wait any longer.
expressions.notes:
더 이상 = (not) any longer
못 = can't

expressions.english: No matter how long I wait, there's no reply.
expressions.notes:
아무리 -이/어/여도- = no matter how + adjective + S + V
답장 = reply

expressions.english: The day I've been waiting for has come. The day has arrived.
expressions.notes: 기다리고 기다리던 = N + that + S + was eagerly looking forward to

expressions.english: I read a book while I was waiting for my friends.
expressions.notes:
-을 is a suffix used to indicate plural.
-(으)면서 = while + S + V-ing
책 = book
읽다 = to read

expressions.english: Tomorrow is the picnic day that I have been eagerly awaiting.
expressions.notes:
내일 = tomorrow
소풍 = picnic
날 = day

expressions.korean: 한참을 기다려도 안 와서, 전화를 해 봤어요.
expressions.english: I'm calling you because I've waited for a long time and you are still not here.
expressions.notes:
-이/어/여도 = even though; no matter how much + S + V
안 = not
오다 = to come
-이/어/여서 = because, since
전화 = phone call; telephone
전화를 하다 = to make a phone call
-이/어/여 보다 = to try + V-ing

expressions.korean: 끝까지 기다려 보세요. 좋은 일이 있을 거예요.
expressions.english: Wait it out. Good things will happen.
expressions.notes:
-(으)세요 = (polite) imperative
좋다 = to be good, likable; to be desirable; to be nice; to like
좋은 = the adjective form of 좋다.
일 = work; task; thing; stuff; occasion
-ㄹ(으)ㄹ 것이다 = will; be going to + V

expressions.korean: 기다리지 말고 주무세요. 저 늦게 들어올 거예요.
expressions.english: Don't wait for me and please go to bed. I will come home late.
expressions.notes:
주무시다 = (honorific) to sleep
저 = (polite) I; me
늦다 = to be late
늦게 = is the adverb form of 늦다.
들어오다 = to come in

expressions.korean: 추우니까 밖에서 기다리지 말고 안에서 기다려요.
expressions.english: Since it's cold outside, don't wait outside, wait inside instead.
expressions.notes:
춥다 = to be cold
-(으)니까 = since, because
밖 = outside

expressions.korean: 줄을 서서 제 차례를 기다리고 있었는데 누가 새치기를 했어요.
expressions.english: I was waiting in line for my turn, and someone cut in line.
expressions.notes:
줄 = line
서다 = to stand; to stop
줄을 서다 = to wait in line
제 = (polite) my; I
누가 = who; someone (subject)
새치기를 하다 = to cut in line
